article.description: — Zach Edey had a career-high 32 points and 17 rebounds to help the Memphis Grizzlies hold off the Sacramento Kings 115-107 on Sunday night.
article.headline: Zach Edey has career-high 32 points to lead Grizzlies past Kings 115-107

images.caption: Memphis Grizzlies vs. Sacramento Kings: Game Highlights
images.name: Memphis Grizzlies vs. Sacramento Kings: Game Highlights

article.linkText: Zach Edey has career-high 32 points to lead Grizzlies past Kings 115-107
article.story: SACRAMENTO, Calif. -- — Zach Edey had a career-high 32 points and 17 rebounds to help the Memphis Grizzlies hold off the Sacramento Kings 115-107 on Sunday night. Edey recorded his third double-double of the season in 29 minutes, shooting 16 of 20 from the floor. He scored six points in the final 3 1/2 minutes to lead the Grizzlies to their fourth straight road win. Cam Spencer had 16 points, Jaylen Wells scored 15 and Jaren Jackson Jr. and Kentavious Caldwell-Pope each had 13 points. Playing without Ja Morant, the Grizzlies — who rallied from double-digit deficits in their previous two games — led much of the way and then had to hold off a late surge by the Kings. Sacramento trailed by 13 in the third quarter before putting together a run to lead 87-83 going into the fourth quarter. Memphis then went on a 14-2 run to begin the fourth, and trailed 101-99 before Edey closed it out down the stretch. DeMar DeRozan scored 23 and made three 3-pointers for the Kings. Malik Monk scored 21. Zach LaVine added 19 points coming off a season-high 34 in a loss to the Utah Jazz on Friday. Edey only shot one free throw, and nearly had his double-double by halftime. Up next Grizzlies: At San Antonio on Tuesday night. Kings: At Houston on Wednesday night. ------ AP NBA: https://apnews.com/NBA

images.name: Luka Doncic [1296x729]

images.caption: LeBron James and the Lakers are on a roll as March winds down.
images.name: LeBron James  [1296x729]

images.alt: Victor Wembanyama #1 of the San Antonio Spurs handles the ball during the game against the Detroit Pistons on March 5, 2026 at the Frost Bank Center in San Antonio, Texas.
images.caption: Victor Wembanyama and the Spurs continue to see their championship odds shift.
images.name: Victor Wembanyama [1296x729]

images.alt: Jayson Tatum #0 of the Boston Celtics celebrates after scoring against the Dallas Mavericks during the second quarter at TD Garden on March 06, 2026 in Boston, Massachusetts.

images.alt: Oklahoma City Thunder guard Shai Gilgeous-Alexander (2) against the Phoenix Suns in the first half at Mortgage Matchup Center.

images.name: Shai Gilgeous-Alexander [1296x729]

images.alt: Cleveland Cavaliers guard Donovan Mitchell (45) against the Phoenix Suns in the first half at Mortgage Matchup Center.

images.name: Donovan Mitchell [1296x729]

images.alt: Jaylen Brown #7 of the Boston Celtics shoots against the Portland Trail Blazers at TD Garden on January 26, 2026 in Boston, Massachusetts.
images.caption: The Celtics now have shorter odds to win the NBA title than the Spurs, Rockets, Knicks and Pistons.

images.caption: Jamal Murray has led the Nuggets while Nikola Jokic recovers from injury.
